mission.title: Install and Configure k0s (Zero Friction Kubernetes)
mission.description: k0s is a lightweight, all-inclusive Kubernetes distribution designed for ease of use and quick deployment. It is ideal for users looking to set up Kubernetes clusters on various environments, including cloud, bare metal, and edge devices. The key trade-off is that while it simplifies installation, users may miss out on some advanced features available in more complex distributions.

steps.title: Install the k0s binary
steps.description:
This step installs the k0s binary on your system, which is required to create and manage your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

curl -sSL https://github.com/k0sproject/k0s/releases/download/v1.31.0+k0s.0/k0s-v1.31.0+k0s.0-linux-amd64 -o /usr/local/bin/k0s

chmod +x /usr/local/bin/k0s

```

> Download the k0s binary.
> Make the k0s binary executable.

steps.title: Initialize the k0s controller
steps.description:
This command initializes the k0s controller, which will manage the Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

k0s init --enable-worker

```

> Initialize the controller and enable worker nodes.

steps.title: Start the k0s controller
steps.description:
This command starts the k0s controller, allowing it to manage the Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

k0s start

```

> Start the k0s controller.

steps.title: Verify the cluster is running
steps.description:
This step checks that the Kubernetes cluster is up and running by verifying the nodes.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster.

uninstall.title: Uninstall k0s
uninstall.description:
This step will remove the k0s installation and all associated data. Be aware that this will delete all Kubernetes resources managed by k0s.

```bash

k0s stop

rm -rf /var/lib/k0s

```

> Stop the k0s controller.
> Remove all k0s data.

upgrade.title: Upgrade k0s
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your cluster configuration. This step will upgrade the k0s binary and restart the controller.

```bash

k0s stop

curl -sSL https://github.com/k0sproject/k0s/releases/download/v1.31.0+k0s.0/k0s-v1.31.0+k0s.0-linux-amd64 -o /usr/local/bin/k0s

chmod +x /usr/local/bin/k0s

k0s start

kubectl get nodes

```

> Stop the k0s controller before upgrading.
> Download the new k0s binary.
> Make the new k0s binary executable.
> Restart the k0s controller.
> Verify that the nodes are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'k0s: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The k0s binary is not installed or not in the PATH.

**Fix:**
Ensure the k0s binary is installed and located in /usr/local/bin.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to start k0s controller'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The k0s controller may already be running or there are configuration issues.

**Fix:**
Check if the controller is already running with 'k0s status' and resolve any configuration issues.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl get nodes returns no nodes
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The k0s controller may not be running or not initialized properly.

**Fix:**
Run 'k0s start' to ensure the controller is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'k0s init failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient permissions or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Run the command with sudo or ensure all dependencies are installed.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for multi-node clusters and improved networking capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for upgrading from v1.30.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get nodes' showing the nodes in the Ready state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure your system meets the minimum resource requirements and has kubectl installed.
